name: california-imperial
description: Imperial County civic data from official county government websites

config.description: Imperial County civic data from official county government websites

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract scheduled and past Imperial County Board of Supervisors meetings — meeting date, time, location, agenda PDF link, and minutes link when available. Each item should yield one Meeting record with externalId (date + body slug), title, body ('Imperial County Board of Supervisors'), scheduledAt (combined date + time), location, agendaUrl, minutesUrl.
hints: Imperial County Board of Supervisors publishes meeting agendas at this URL
Use these EXACT field names: externalId, title, body, scheduledAt, location, agendaUrl, minutesUrl
Past meetings and upcoming meetings may live on the same page or on tabbed/paginated sections — capture both when present

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract Imperial County Board of Supervisors members — name, district number, party affiliation if listed, photo URL, contact info (phone, email, district office address), and profile page link. Construct externalId as 'california-imperial-supervisor-{district}' using the district number.
hints: externalId: construct as 'california-imperial-supervisor-{district}' using the district number
The HTML contains a nested navigation menu structure with 'menu-item' elements containing 'a' tags with 'brz-span' children holding the district names (e.g., 'District 1').
District links are located within the sub-menu of the 'Board Home' menu item, specifically in 'li' elements with 'menu-item-type-post_type' containing 'a' tags with hrefs like '/district-1/'.
The 'brz-span' element text provides the district name (e.g., 'District 1'), which can be used to derive the 'district' field value.
The 'detailUrl' can be directly extracted from the 'href' attribute of the 'a' tags within the district menu items.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract current and past ballot measures for Imperial County — measure identifier (letter/number), full title, election date, sponsoring jurisdiction, measure type (bond, tax, initiative, ordinance, charter amendment, etc.), summary description, and official outcome (passed/failed) where available.
hints: Imperial County Registrar of Voters publishes ballot measures at this URL

Extract current and past ballot measures — measure letter/number, title, election date, jurisdiction (county, city, district), measure type (bond, tax, initiative, etc.), short description, and outcome when available
Use these EXACT field names: externalId, title, electionDate, jurisdiction, measureType, description, outcome

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract campaign finance disclosure filings for Imperial County — committee name, filer type (candidate/measure committee), election, total contributions received, total expenditures made, and top contributors with amounts.
hints: Imperial County campaign finance disclosure portal
Extract campaign finance filings — committee name, candidate or measure supported/opposed, filing date, total contributions, total expenditures, top contributors (name, amount), and reporting period
Use these EXACT field names: committeeId, committeeName, candidateOrMeasure, filingDate, totalContributions, totalExpenditures, reportingPeriod
NOTE: UNCERTAIN — No NetFile portal confirmed for Imperial. Verify on elections.imperialcounty.org.
